lessonTitle: Targeting Pathobiology of Inflammation and Hb S Polymerization in Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: Identify promising options for the treatment of Sickle Cell Disease
List options for targeting the pathobiology of Sickle Cell Disease and inflammation
Discuss options for targeting the pathobiology of Sickle Cell Disease and HbS polymerization

slides.title: Targeting Pathobiology of Inflammation in Sickle Cell Disease

data.html: We are off to a good start! We have looked at how VOEs could be treated at home and the role of Crizanlizumab in attacking the pathobiology of VOEs in SCD. Now we're ready to move into trials of medications targeting inflammation in SCD.
Photo credit: Salinas Cisneros G & Thein SL (2000)

L-glutamine

One of the contributing factors to the pathophysiology of sickle cell pain is oxidative stress. L-glutamine, as an oral therapy, contributes to increased proportions of the reduced nicotinamide adenine dinucleotides phosphates (NADPH) in sickle cell erythrocytes, which in turn reduces oxidative stress.

FDA Approved in 2017
Phase 3 Trial of L-Glutamine in Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: Reduces oxidative stress by synthesizing NADPH and glutathione
2 RCTs - Phase II and Phase III, placebo controlled
Phase II: non-significant reduction in VOC
Phase III: painful crises event composite was non-significant at 24 weeks but was significantly reduced at 48 weeks
~$30,000 USD/year for BID powder
230 patients aged 5+, mostly HbSS
Mostly 2-5 crises in previous year (more 6-9 crises in placebo group)
~66% in both groups received HU
36% did not complete in L glutamine arm and 24% in the placebo arm
Lower cumulative pain crises at 48 weeks
Longer time to first crisis

data.html: Photo credit: Niihara, Y., et al., (2018)

data.bubble_text: Now let's look at medications that target the multiprotein, inflammasone.

data.html:
Canakinumab (Ilaris)
A randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind trial of Canakinumab in children and young adults with sickle cell anemia (Rees et al., 2022)

Inflammasome, a multiprotein oligmer, is activated when red blood cells lyse and release their intravascular contents, leading to the maturation and secretion of proinflammatory cytokines, such as interleukin-1b (IL-1β).

Study Hypothesis:
Reduction in inflammatory and clinical disease activity by blocking IL-1β by Canakinumab in patients with SCD.

Mechanism:
Selective blockade of IL-1b to reduce inflammation.

Evidence:
Phase II placebo controlled randomized control trial of 49 participants.

items.html: Patients randomized to Canakinumab or placebo
Study visits every 4 weeks up to the final blinded assessment at Week 24
Option to receive open label Canakinumab every 4 weeks until Week 48
Final study outcome assessments made in Week 52

data.html: Number of patients at risk for being rehospitalized is noted in the red and blue numbers, for Canakinumab and placebo, respectively.

items.html: Pre-specified reduction in pain was not met
Reductions in markers of inflammation, occurrence of SCD-related adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs)
Reduced hemolysis (less reticulocytes, bilirubin)
Reduced number and duration of hospitalizations

data.html: Now let's dive into the next trial: The Randomized, Doubleblind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 1b Crosswalk-A Trial Evaluating The Safety Of Crovalimab For The Management Of Acute Uncomplicated Vasoocclusive Episodes (VOEs) In Patients With Sickle Cell Disease (Bartolucci et. al., 2022)
Although most patients stay home to manage their VOEs, >70% of emergency department visits and > 90% of hospitalizations are VOE-related.

When sickle cell patients are in acute pain, there is activation of the complement pathway, which contributes to the debilitating impact of VOEs. Crovalimab has shown rapid and sustained inhibition of the complement pathway with promising safety and efficacy. It is a self-administered subcutaneous injection.

Lets take a brief look at an image of the complex complement system. SCD triggers the Alternative Pathway, which leads to inflammation, cell lysis, and cell activation through 2 enzymes, c3 and c5 convertase.

The complement pathway can be targeted with Crovalimab:
Novel, engineered, anti-complement C5 monoclonal antibody

Mechanism:
Complement blockade (C5) which reduces ongoing inflammation in the setting of VOC/endothelial activation/hemolysis

items.html: Number of VOEs
Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS) - Fatigue score from baseline to Week 49

data.html: Expected: Summer 2024

slides.title: Targeting Pathobiology of HbS Polymerization in SCD

data.html: We have learnt about innovations in targeting inflammation in the pathobiology of VOEs with Canakinumab and Crovalimab. Now we will move into an area where more research has been done. Considering the pathobiology of HbS polymerization in SCD, we will review approaches that aim to improve HbF production and/or improve oxygen affinity in red cells, thereby disrupting HbS polymerization.
Photo credit: (Salinas Cisneros G, & Thein SL., 2020)

Let's look at ways of improving oxygen affinity of hemoglobin to start.

Voxelotor
FDA approved in 2019

Mechanism:
Hemoglobin S polymerization inhibitor, increased oxygen affinity resulting in increased red cell survival and reduced hemolysis

Evidence:
HOPE trial (Phase III) looking for hemoglobin increase (VOC was a secondary outcome)

items.html: Hb increased by at least 10 points in the high dose (89%), low dose (72%) compared to placebo (25%) at 72 weeks
274 patients randomized, aged 12+, mostly HbSS
Patients with recent pain crises or transfusions within 60 days
Increase in hematologic parameters occurred by 2 weeks and persisted
No significant difference in VOC
Similar rate of adverse events

data.html: Let's move on now to a Phase 1 trial.

items.title: Phase 1 Trial
items.html:
• Focused on minimal doses for depletion of DNA Methyl Transferase 1 (DNMT1), without cytotoxicity

Mechanism

  • DNMT1 is the enzyme that silences HbF from infancy onwards
  • Decitabine interferes with the function of DNMT1
  • Tetrahydrouridine inhibits enzyme that rapidly deactivates Decitabine

items.title: Sample
items.html:
• 5 groups of 5 patients randomized 3:2 decitabine to placebo
  • Treated 2x/week x 8 weeks with 4 weeks of follow up

items.title: Results
items.html:
• Increased Hb 12-19 g/L (P=0.01)
  • HbF increased by 4-9%, less hemolysis
  • No significant difference in treatment vs placebo adverse events

data.question: Match the word at the top with the matching word or phrase below: Canakinumab (Ilaris)
data.feedback_correct: Canakinumab • Inflammasome, multi-protein complex • Studied in children and young adults with SCD • No change in pain

data.question: Match the word at the top with the matching word or phrase below: Crovalimab
data.feedback_correct: Crovalimab • Inhibits complement pathway • Subcutaneous injection administered by patient • Complement 5 blockade

data.question: Match the word at the top with the matching word or phrase below: Voxelotor
data.feedback_correct: Voxelotor • Hemoglobin S polymerization inhibitor • Hemoglobin increases • No significant difference in VOEs

data.html:
Let's switch gears and look at a drug showing great potential in increasing oxygen affinity of hemoglobin. Let's find out more about this drug, Mitapivat.

Photo credit: https://www.bio-connect.nl/news/fda-new-approvals-mitapivat-pyrukynd/

items.html: Activator of pyruvate kinase (involved in RBC metabolism)
Increases adenosine triphosphate (ATP) which improves RBC survival
Decreases 2,3 diphosphoglycerate (2, 3-DPG) which increases oxygen affinity of Hb

data.html:
Phase 2 double-blind period

Sample:
2 dose levels of Mitapivat, 50mg and 100mg, were evaluated against placebo — 12 week period

Primary endpoints:
Hb response, defined as a ≥1.0 g/dL increase in average Hb concentration over weeks 10–12 compared with baseline; Adverse events
Photo credit: Idowu M, et. al., 2023

items.html: Significant increase in Hb response
46.2% (12/26) of pts in the 50 mg BID arm
50.0% (13/26) in the 100 mg BID arm
3.7% (1/27) of pts in the PBO arm

data.html: Photo credit: Idowu M, et. al., 2023
We have one more study that is focused on improving hemoglobin oxygen affinity. Get ready to learn about GBT021601.

items.html: GBT021601 stabilizes hemoglobin (Hb) in the oxygenated state to inhibit HbS polymerization
Compared to voxelotor, GBT021601 has a longer half-life and potential to achieve higher Hb occupancies at lower doses
Part A, dose-finding study of GBT021601 in adult participants, of the Phase 2 study are presented
Age 18–65 years
Documented SCD (HbSS or HbSβ0)
Hb 5.5–10.5 g/dL during screening
HU or L-glutamine permitted
≤10 VOCs within 12 months of screening
Participants randomly assigned 1:1 to GBT021601 100 mg or 150 mg
Both groups received a loading dose twice daily for 4 days
Once-daily maintenance doses through to Week 12
Change in hemoglobin from baseline at Week 12
Hb response (increase from baseline >1 g/dL)
Change from baseline in hemolysis markers at Week 12, PK, PD
Safety
Mean increase in Hb from baseline was 2.67g/dL for the 100-mg group (n=12) and 3.17g/dL for the 150-mg group
1/35 patients (2.9%) had an AE leading to treatment discontinuation due to sepsis
8/35 patients (22.9%) reported ≥1 treatment-related AE, all were grade 1 or 2
One death, considered unrelated to GBT021601, occurred on study
Also of interest, there was a reduction in adherent cells, which could correlate with a lower probability of a VOC event.

data.html:
Conclusion:
GBT021601 taken as a loading dose followed by daily doses for 12 weeks was well tolerated. Pain episodes did not increase, even though there were large increases in mean Hb levels. Data from Part A support GBT021601 as a potential treatment for individuals with SCD, still requiring ongoing clinical development.

slides.title: Fetal Hemoglobin Induction In the Treatment of β-Hemoglobinopathies

items.html: Hydroxyurea has the most evidence and remains the backbone of treatment for SCD
Hydroxyurea requires tailored dosing and haematologic monitoring
Fetal globin induction is the basis of most gene therapy options (lovo-cel, exa-cel, reni-cel, etc.)
Most known HbF regulators, being transcription factors, are historically 'undruggable'

data.html:
Targeted Degradation of the Wiz Transcription Factor for Gamma Globin De-Repression
Cereblon (CRBN) is a naturally existing protein degradation machinery in cells. Phenotypic screen of CRBN-biased library identified dWIZ-1, a small molecule HbF inducer.

items.html: WIZ (transcription factor) dependent HbF induction by CRISPR-Cas9 validated by CRISPR knockout (Figure B)
WIZ degradation leads to ↓ WIZ binding in β-globin locus which leads to ↓ H3K9me2, ↑ γ-globin mRNA, and ↑ HbF
dWIZ-2 degrades WIZ and induces HbF in CD34+ derived erythroblasts from sickle cell disease donors and mouse xenotransplantation model
Success of dWIZ-2 opens up a new paradigm in therapeutic targeting of transcription factors
Objective: to elucidate the mechanism of how cell cycle speed impacts γ-globin expression in erythroid cells
In HUDEP-2 cells and primary erythroid cells, slower cell cycle speed produce higher γ-globin expression

data.html: CDKN1b (p27Kip1) overexpression resulted in:

items.html: Increased F-cells, from ~6% up to ~20% (p= 0.024)
Approximately 14-fold increase in γ-globin mRNA levels (p= 0.026)
Reduction in beta-globin mRNA level
Reduction in BCL11A mRNA level to approximately 65% of normal
Increased expression of 8 Cyclin Dependent Kinase Inhibitors with CRISPR activation (CRISPRa) in HUDEP-2 cells
Same was observed in erythroid cells differentiated from CD34+ HSPCs
Modulating cell cycle speed may become a HbF induction target in the future

slides.title: Upregulation of HbF by a DNMT1 Depleter - AB1

items.html: γ-globin gene transcription is silenced after birth via a repressor complex including DNA methyltransferase 1 (DNMT1)
AB1 is a potent non-cytotoxic agent which depletes DNMT1 to induce HbF in sickle erythroid progenitors (Li et al., unpublished data)

data.html:
The figure below depicts the β-like globin genes' chromosomal order, along with the cis-acting sites that consists of binding sites for the trans acting elements.

The following table gives you a bit more detail about each element and their function. Take note that BCL11A is a major repressor of γ-globin gene (HBG2, HBG1) transcription.
Photo credit: Habara et. al., 2017

data.heading: In SCD Townes mice model, AB1:

items.html: Increases γ-globin mRNA and F-cells
Decreases sickle cell phenotype in blood
Improves splenic hematopoiesis
Canakinumab has shown efficacy in reducing pain crisis and hospitalizations.
Voxelotor has been shown to Increase hematologic parameters, with no significant difference in VOC
dWIZ-2 is degrades WIZ and induces HbF in CD34+ derived erythroblasts from sickle cell disease donors, which is very promising
Mitapivat has shown great potential by increasing oxygen affinity of hemoglobin
